customer_anxiety_profile: Nancy is curious and engaged but explicitly non-committal ('I'm not gonna commit to anything right away'). She is a first-time solar inquirer who needs program mechanics explained before she will agree to a meeting. She is not hostile — she is cautious and information-seeking. Her bill pain is real ($380 winter peak, $170-190 summer) and she has already internalized the bill-swap concept.
unresolved_concerns: Does the solar bill rate get reviewed annually or is it fixed for the term?
How does the solar bill amount get determined — what drives it up or down?
Can a battery be installed without a garage?
What exactly is included in the 'estimate' beyond kWh usage?
sensitive_topics_raised: Nancy has no online Dominion account and no paper bill — bill collection requires either a verbal readback from a mailed statement or a Dominion phone call, which she noted is difficult to reach
exact_promises_rep_made: Holland will text Nancy her contact information
Holland will notate Nancy's questions about annual rate review and bill variability for the specialist
Holland is waiting for Nancy to call Dominion on Monday and send over 12 months of kWh usage
closer_should_know: No appointment was booked. This is a warm soft-callback — Nancy paraphrased the bill-swap concept back unprompted and asked four follow-up questions, which signals genuine interest. The Monday follow-up rep should lead with a direct booking ask (the specialist will answer all four of her open questions on the call) rather than waiting for the bill. If this is an organic lead, book first and collect the bill at the site visit. If paid, offer a locked callback time contingent on bill receipt rather than leaving it open-ended. Nancy's four unresolved questions are booking levers, not obstacles.
